name: Together AI
what_it_does: Fast, scalable inference API for open-source LLMs including Llama, Mixtral, Qwen, and other open models, with an OpenAI-compatible endpoint for easy drop-in replacement.
use_cases: Running open-source LLMs without managing GPU infrastructure
Drop-in replacement for OpenAI API using open models
Cost-effective LLM inference at scale compared to frontier APIs
Fine-tuning open models on custom datasets
Generating embeddings with open embedding models
not_for: Applications requiring proprietary frontier models (GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini)
Real-time streaming with sub-50ms first-token latency requirements
Highly regulated environments requiring on-premise deployment
best_when: An agent needs open-model LLM inference at lower cost than frontier APIs, or when OpenAI compatibility is needed with model flexibility.
avoid_when: You need guaranteed SLAs for production workloads or require frontier-model reasoning quality.

auth.notes: Single API key per account via dashboard. Key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header. No scoped or restricted keys — all keys have full account access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: $1 credit for new users; no ongoing free tier
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-token pricing varies by model — Llama 3.1 8B ~$0.18/M tokens, Llama 3.1 70B ~$0.88/M tokens, Mixtral 8x7B ~$0.60/M tokens
pricing.notes: Significantly cheaper than OpenAI for comparable open models. Serverless (on-demand) and dedicated endpoint options. Dedicated endpoints for high-volume workloads.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; default 60 requests/minute on serverless endpoints. Dedicated endpoints have configurable limits.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: OpenAI-compatible errors for standard cases. Model-specific errors can be inconsistent. Rate limit errors include retry-after headers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys; inference is stateless by nature. Agents must handle retries carefully to avoid duplicate completions.
known_agent_gotchas: Model availability can change without notice — check /models endpoint before hardcoding model IDs
Serverless endpoints may have cold-start latency spikes (multi-second) for infrequently-used models
Context windows vary widely by model — always validate max_tokens against model limits
No request idempotency — network retries can cause duplicate LLM calls and charges
Rate limits are per-account not per-model; concurrent model calls share the same limit bucket
OpenAI compatibility is not 100% — some parameters like logit_bias may be silently ignored

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single monolithic API key with no scoping is a concern for agents — a compromised key exposes all account capabilities. No IP allowlisting in standard tier. SOC2 Type II certified.
